subject: Remove XBox One Wireless Adapter USB IDs from mt76 driver to allow 'xow' driver compatibility.
author: sofakng

upstream.vanilla_quote:
{ USB_DEVICE(0x045e, 0x02e6) },	/* XBox One Wireless Adapter */
{ USB_DEVICE(0x045e, 0x02fe) },	/* XBox One Wireless Adapter */

provenance_doc_ref: docs/patch-provenance.md section 3.4, entry 817ace70b (class D/E); also section 6, entry 0027-mt76x2u-release-xbox-adapter-ids.patch

impact.effect_if_absent: The mt76 driver will bind to Xbox Wireless Dongle USB devices (045e:02e6 and 045e:02fe) before the xone driver can claim them. The mt76 driver treats these as Wi-Fi NICs and will fail to initialize them correctly, preventing wireless Xbox controllers/headsets from functioning. The xone package (BR2_PACKAGE_XONE=y) will be present but unable to bind to these devices.

userspace_coupling.interface: Input device nodes from xone driver; xone provides /dev/input/event* nodes for wireless Xbox controllers/headsets via the GIP (Game Input Protocol) transport layer
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp: all input device access via /dev/input/event*; xone driver exposes input devices for wireless adapters bound via package/xone

notes: CARRY PATCH — Removes USB device IDs 045e:02e6 and 045e:02fe from mt76x2u_device_table[]. These IDs refer to Xbox Wireless Dongle devices (old and new variants), which implement the Xbox GIP (Game Input Protocol) for wireless controllers/headsets — they are NOT Wi-Fi NICs despite the device ID overlap. Vanilla 6.18.38 still claims these IDs in mt76 (drivers/net/wireless/mediatek/mt76/mt76x2/usb.c:26-27), causing the mt76 driver to bind before xone can. The xone driver has its own device table claiming the same IDs (transport/dongle.c in xone codebase: 045e:02e6 'old dongle', 045e:02fe 'new dongle', plus two others). Without this patch, mt76 will win the driver-matching race and the Xbox Wireless Dongle cannot function. Failure is SILENT: the device appears to be recognized but produces no input events. The forward-ported patch (0027-mt76x2u-release-xbox-adapter-ids.patch) is byte-identical removal but adds an explanatory comment to prevent accidental reintroduction during future mt76 syncs. Patch applies cleanly to 6.18.38 with no conflicts. Provenance doc correctly identifies this as a carry (not upstream); xone is carried as BR2_PACKAGE_XONE (a Buildroot out-of-tree driver package sourced from dlundqvist/xone, the actively-maintained fork). Original commit author sofakng, forward-ported by MCF during P1.9 task.
